title: Payroll Services

narrative.buyability: The selected-archetype count is retained as a source-free bridge assumption, but no evidence supports five-year sale availability, buyer access and win, or either completed-and-onboarded capacity constraint.
narrative.commercial_capture: No target-archetype contract, pricing, retention, concentration, or customer-switching dataset was supplied. Commercial retention is kept separate from operational realization and remains MISSING.
narrative.entry_and_exit: No comparable, consistently normalized entry multiple, canonical entry-equivalent year-five state, or downside multiple support was supplied. The multiple-only channel is consequently MISSING.
narrative.executive_view: This packet is deliberately evidence-limited. It retains the frozen full-service payroll-provider archetype and supplies only source-free assumption inputs mandated by the frozen value basis; other company-specific primitives remain MISSING.
narrative.implementation: No evidence described the target's payroll platform, interfaces, authorizations, parallel-run obligations, control checkpoints, or change costs. Operational realization and implementation investment are therefore MISSING.
narrative.operator_survival: No fixed-base service quantities, recognized revenue, pass-through amounts, or year-five operator-controlled quantities were supplied. A Laspeyres survival basket cannot be constructed without inventing baseline primitives.
narrative.revenue_and_pass_through: No target trailing-twelve-month accrual recognized-revenue schedule, client-funds reconciliation, or embedded third-party pass-through evidence was supplied. Client payroll and remittance flows are not substituted for recognized revenue.
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: Company-specific revenue recognition, pass-through, labor cost, technology, retention, supply, valuation, and survival evidence is absent. The assumption basis makes readiness UNPROVEN and requires evidence-first diligence.
narrative.technical_value: The frozen role taxonomy is preserved exactly, but it is a source-free planning taxonomy. It cannot establish actual payroll-provider cash costs or achievable removability without target payroll, contractor, and operating evidence.

scenario_narratives.base: The base scenario does not impute target revenue, operating realization, commercial retention, deal flow, valuation, or operator survival. It is a research boundary statement rather than a company-underwriting case.
scenario_narratives.high: The upside scenario does not treat missing target evidence as favorable. It retains the same frozen archetype and source-free assumption treatment while leaving unsupported primitives unresolved.
scenario_narratives.low: The downside scenario preserves only the frozen archetype coverage bridge and bounded source-free cash/removability assumptions. All other unobserved primitives remain missing; no adverse economic value is fabricated from missing evidence.

sources.title: U.S. Census Bureau NAICS profile: 541214 Payroll Services
sources.what_it_supports: The frozen industry authority identifies the NAICS scope and selected payroll-service archetype; it does not supply target-company economic primitives.

n_band.rationale: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $98,054 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541214); class <5: 2,110 firms, avg revenue/firm $581,493 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $91,004 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 662 firms, avg revenue/firm $919,816 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $143,951 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 480 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,588,508 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $248,602 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 741 firms, avg revenue/firm $4,599,022 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $719,747 -> out of $1-10M band; class 100-499: 297 firms, avg revenue/firm $13,560,636 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $2,122,240 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 194 firms, avg revenue/firm $89,119,103 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $13,947,140 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 297
caveats: Normalized dataset value lacks MED/HIGH support and is not promoted to DIRECT.

inclusion_criteria: The establishment's primary activity is payroll processing within NAICS 541214, rather than accounting, bookkeeping, billing, staffing, or co-employment.
The establishment offers a bundled full-service payroll product that includes payroll calculation together with payroll tax filing or reporting and related pay-cycle administration.

The establishment may also sell individual payroll modules or ancillary time, attendance, human-resources, or benefits-administration services, provided payroll services remain its primary classified activity.
exclusion_criteria: Payroll-services establishments that sell only individual payroll services and do not offer a bundled full-service payroll product.
CPA offices and non-CPA firms whose primary offering combines payroll with accounting, bookkeeping, or billing services.
Professional employer organizations, employers of record, staffing firms, and other businesses that become or assert an employer role for client workers.
Software publishers or data processors that license payroll technology but do not provide the payroll service described by NAICS 541214.
target_archetype.selection_basis: The alternatives are mutually exclusive by full-service-product incidence. The selected base is 276 firms versus 17 for the next-largest archetype, using the normalized snapshot's 297-firm band estimate and the official 2022 product-incidence shares. The supported ranges do not overlap (selected low 245 versus alternative high 42), so the largest-base rule selects full-service-offering without selection uncertainty. The base enumeration covers 293 / 297 = 98.65% of the estimated band; its low endpoint is 245 / 297 = 82.49%. Range endpoints are marginal bridge bounds and are not intended to be added as a joint scenario.

cva_reconciliation.controllable_value_added_boundary: Begin with recognized payroll-service and ancillary-service revenue attributable to the selected full-service provider. Exclude client custodial, trust, settlement, and remittance cash flows before the calculation because they are not revenue. Deduct only an approved category that is embedded in recognized revenue, documented as contractually or economically owed to an outside provider, and not controlled as value added by the operator. Payroll platform, hosting, employee, and operator-controlled contractor expenses remain inside controllable value-added revenue and are not denominator deductions.

numerator_costs.accounting_basis: Source-free TTM cash operating-cost planning amount in the same units as recognized revenue.
Source-free TTM cash operating-cost planning amount in the same units as recognized revenue, excluding pass-through.
